sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting observations:
1. First RIGHT: Green stayed at [[40,12],[41,13]] - no change (it was already there from previous RIGHT)
2. Second RIGHT: board_changed=False - green couldn't move further right (at container edge)
3. UP: Green moved to [[38,12],[39,13]] - it moved UP by 2 rows but stayed at the same columns! It didn't swap with a divider block this time!
Wait, this is different from before. Previously when green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and I pressed UP, it swapped with the divider block at [[38,32],[39,33]]. But now when green was at [[40,12],[41,13]] and I pressed UP, it just moved up to [[38,12],[39,13]] without swapping.
So the behavior of UP depends on the position of the green pixel:
- When at [[40,10],[41,11]] (left side of container), UP swaps with divider block
- When at [[40,12],[41,13]] (right side of container), UP just moves up within the container
This suggests that UP has different behaviors depending on position:
- If the object is at the right edge of its container (adjacent to the divider), UP might swap with the divider
- If the object is not at the right edge, UP just moves it up within the container
Wait, but [[40,12],[41,13]] IS at the right edge of the container (container goes to col 13). Let me reconsider.
Actually, looking more carefully:
- When green was at [[40,10],[41,11]], UP moved it to [[38,32],[39,33]] (swapped with divider)
- When green was at [[40,12],[41,13]], UP moved it to [[38,12],[39,13]] (just moved up)
Hmm, but the green pixel is now at [[38,12],[39,13]] which is still inside the container. The container boundary changed.
Wait, I need to reconsider. Maybe UP always moves the object up within the container, and the "swap with divider" behavior only happens when the object reaches a certain position?
Let me check: when green was at [[40,10],[41,11]] and I pressed UP, it went to [[38,32],[39,33]]. That's not just moving up - that's a huge jump to col 32-33. So something special happened.
But now when green was at [[40,12],[41,13]] and I pressed UP, it went to [[38,12],[39,13]]. That's just moving up 2 rows.
The difference might be:
- When at col 10-11 (left side), UP triggers a swap with divider
- When at col 12-13 (right side), UP just moves up
Or maybe:
- The first time I pressed UP, the green pixel was at a position where there was no space above it within the container, so it swapped with the divider
- This time, there WAS space above it within the container, so it just moved up
Let me check the container shape. The container (node 32) boundary was [[38, 8], [38, 13], [39, 13], [39, 12], [40, 11], [41, 11], [42, 12], [42, 13], [43, 13], [43, 8]]. This is an irregular shape. The green pixel at [[40,12],[41,13]] has space above it at [[38,12],[39,13]] which is within the container.
So the rule might be:
- UP/DOWN moves the object within the container if there's space
- If there's no space (object is at the edge), UP/DOWN swaps with the divider block
Let me test this by pressing UP again from [[38,12],[39,13]]. If there's no space above, it should swap with a divider block.
Actually wait, let me re-examine. The green pixel is now at [[38,12],[39,13]]. The container's top is at row 38. So the green pixel is at the top edge of the container. If I press UP again, there's no space above within the container, so it should swap with a divider block.
Let me try UP again and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Green moved UP to [[38,12],[39,13]] - just moved up within container, no swap
# This is different from before when it swapped with divider
# Hypothesis: UP/DOWN moves within container if space exists, swaps with divider at edges

# Green is at top edge of container now (row 38). Try UP again - should swap with divider?
action(['UP'])
print(f"After UP: {last_action_result}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = {n['id']: n for n in seg['nodes']}

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['id'] == 32:
        print(f"Container 32: boundary={n['boundary']} children={n.get('children')}")

# Check divider blocks near row 38
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] == 4:
        print(f"Divider: id={n['id']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>

UP/DOWN at container edges swaps with divider blocks. At [[38,12],[39,13]] now.", "goal_model": "Move green pixel through divider to right side? Or rearrange objects?", "recent_findings": ["UP behavior depends on position - moves within container if space, swaps at edge", "Green at [[38,12],[39,13]] after UP from right edge"], "open_questions": ["What happens when green reaches divider via UP at top edge?", "What is the goal?"], "current_plan": "Press UP again from top edge to trigger divider swap", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["MOUSE on yellow - no selection change", "2nd RIGHT - no movement (at edge)"]}}
</parameter>
